allergens: gluten (buns, wonton wrappers, onion ring batter)
eggs (possible in slaw dressing and batter)

serving.description: 1 sharing platter (4 sliders, 4 wantons, edamame, slaw, onion rings)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full sharing platter comprising four Beet Your Meet sliders, four cheese wantons, a portion of edamame, coleslaw, and onion rings, estimated at approximately 700 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods: veggie burger patty (FDC 2103718), wonton (FDC 168927), edamame (FDC 168411), coleslaw (FDC 172944), onion rings (FDC 172135); weighted composite per described platter composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry exists for this specific restaurant platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for each component: veggie/beetroot-style burger patty, cheese-filled wonton (fried), edamame, coleslaw with dressing, and battered onion rings. Approximate weight distribution: sliders 280 g (4 × 70 g), wantons 80 g (4 × 20 g), edamame 80 g, slaw 120 g, onion rings 140 g. Per-100g values derived from this composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed vegetarian meal analogues estimated from component GI values: burger bun (GI ~71), legumes/edamame (GI ~18), coleslaw vegetables (GI ~15), battered onion rings (GI ~76), wonton wrappers (GI ~60); weighted composite GI ~52
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (component references) and Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific platter. Composite GI estimated by weighting component GI values (University of Sydney GI Database) against approximate carbohydrate contributions per component: slider buns contribute highest carbohydrate load (GI ~71), edamame low GI (~18), coleslaw vegetables very low GI (~15), onion rings high GI (~76), wonton wrappers moderate GI (~60). Weighted composite GI ≈ 52 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 150.5) / 100 ≈ 78.3, rounded to 78.0. Serving carbohydrate estimated at 700 g × 0.215 = 150.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 380 mg per 100 g; full platter estimated at 2,660 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a full serving of this platter may provide sodium approaching or exceeding the recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg; portion control and requesting reduced-sodium preparation where possible is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Composite GI of approximately 52 (low) but high total glycemic load (~78) due to large serving size with significant carbohydrate from buns, wonton wrappers, and onion rings
type2_diabetes.guidance: Despite a low composite GI, the large carbohydrate load from buns, wonton wrappers, and battered onion rings in a full platter makes portion control essential for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; sharing the platter or limiting high-GI components such as onion rings is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from four-cheese wantons and deep-fried onion rings; elevated sodium from processed components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat from cheese-filled wantons and deep-fried onion rings alongside high sodium content warrants moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease; choosing smaller portions and pairing with the edamame and slaw components is preferable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lick My Greens is a vegetarian sharing platter served at Riverside Buns, Boat Quay, Singapore, featuring Taiwanese-influenced street food components. The platter comprises four Beet Your Meet sliders — plant-based patties made with beetroot and legumes served in soft buns — four four-cheese wantons (deep-fried wonton parcels filled with a blend of cheeses), a portion of steamed edamame, creamy coleslaw, and crispy battered onion rings. The dish is entirely vegetarian but not vegan due to dairy in the wantons and potential egg in the slaw dressing and batter. Texturally, the platter offers contrast between the soft, juicy sliders, the crispy fried wantons and onion rings, the tender edamame, and the cool, creamy slaw. Nutritionally, the platter is moderately energy-dense at approximately 198 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from the plant patties and edamame (7.2 g/100 g), moderate fat (9.8 g/100 g) including saturated fat from cheese, and significant carbohydrate (21.5 g/100 g) from buns, wonton wrappers, and onion ring batter. Dietary fibre is moderate at 3.0 g/100 g. Sodium is elevated at approximately 380 mg/100 g, making the full platter a high-sodium meal. The composite glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low category), though the glycemic load per full serving is high (~78) due to the large carbohydrate mass. Clinically, this dish is suitable for vegetarians seeking a satisfying shared meal but requires portion awareness for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The edamame component provides isoflavones and plant protein, while the beetroot-based patty contributes dietary nitrates and antioxidants. This dish is representative of Singapore's fusion vegetarian dining scene, blending Taiwanese bun culture with Western-style sliders and Asian dim sum elements.
common_user_queries: What is Lick My Greens at Riverside Buns?
Calories in Lick My Greens platter

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific restaurant platter. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central component entries. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is medium due to estimation methodology and the inherent variability of restaurant preparation.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entries used for this composite estimation. The platter is sold as a single menu item at SGD 50.00 at Riverside Buns, Boat Quay, Singapore (field survey data listing). The food_name has been expanded to 'Lick My Greens Vegetarian Platter' to provide a more canonical and descriptive English identity while preserving the original menu name 'Lick My Greens' as the primary alias. Multilingual names are descriptive translations reflecting the nature of the dish (green vegetarian platter) rather than transliterations of the English menu name, as the dish does not have established native-language names. The cuisine tag 'Taiwanese' is preserved from the source as the restaurant (Riverside Buns) identifies with Taiwanese bun culture, though the dish is served in Singapore and incorporates fusion elements. GI category is 'low' (GI = 52, which is ≤ 55 per Veritas threshold rules). Glycemic load per serving is calculated as (52 × 150.5) / 100 ≈ 78.0, where 150.5 g is the estimated carbohydrate content of the full 700 g platter.
